papers.title: A Cyber Threat Intelligence Sharing Scheme based on Federated Learning for Network Intrusion Detection
papers.abstract: The uses of Machine Learning (ML) in detection of network attacks have been effective when designed and evaluated in a single organisation. However, it has been very challenging to design an ML-based detection system by utilising heterogeneous network data samples originating from several sources. This is mainly due to privacy concerns and the lack of a universal format of datasets. In this paper, we propose a collaborative federated learning scheme to address these issues. The proposed framework allows multiple organisations to join forces in the design, training, and evaluation of a robust ML-based network intrusion detection system. The threat intelligence scheme utilises two critical aspects for its application; the availability of network data traffic in a common format to allow for the extraction of meaningful patterns across data sources. Secondly, the adoption of a federated learning mechanism to avoid the necessity of sharing sensitive users' information between organisations. As a result, each organisation benefits from other organisations cyber threat intelligence while maintaining the privacy of its data internally. The model is trained locally and only the updated weights are shared with the remaining participants in the federated averaging process. The framework has been designed and evaluated in this paper by using two key datasets in a NetFlow format known as NF-UNSW-NB15-v2 and NF-BoT-IoT-v2. Two other common scenarios are considered in the evaluation process; a centralised training method where the local data samples are shared with other organisations and a localised training method where no threat intelligence is shared. The results demonstrate the efficiency and effectiveness of the proposed framework by designing a universal ML model effectively classifying benign and intrusive traffic originating from multiple organisations without the need for local data exchange.

papers.title: GowFed -- A novel Federated Network Intrusion Detection System
papers.abstract: Network intrusion detection systems are evolving into intelligent systems that perform data analysis while searching for anomalies in their environment. Indeed, the development of deep learning techniques paved the way to build more complex and effective threat detection models. However, training those models may be computationally infeasible in most Edge or IoT devices. Current approaches rely on powerful centralized servers that receive data from all their parties - violating basic privacy constraints and substantially affecting response times and operational costs due to the huge communication overheads. To mitigate these issues, Federated Learning emerged as a promising approach, where different agents collaboratively train a shared model, without exposing training data to others or requiring a compute-intensive centralized infrastructure. This work presents GowFed, a novel network threat detection system that combines the usage of Gower Dissimilarity matrices and Federated averaging. Different approaches of GowFed have been developed based on state-of the-art knowledge: (1) a vanilla version; and (2) a version instrumented with an attention mechanism. Furthermore, each variant has been tested using simulation oriented tools provided by TensorFlow Federated framework. In the same way, a centralized analogous development of the Federated systems is carried out to explore their differences in terms of scalability and performance - across a set of designed experiments/scenarios. Overall, GowFed intends to be the first stepping stone towards the combined usage of Federated Learning and Gower Dissimilarity matrices to detect network threats in industrial-level networks.

papers.title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection In Internet Of Vehicles Through Federated Learning
papers.abstract: Federated learning is a technique of decentralized machine learning. that allows multiple parties to collaborate and learn a shared model without sharing their raw data. Our paper proposes a federated learning framework for intrusion detection in Internet of Vehicles (IOVs) using the CIC-IDS 2017 dataset. The proposed framework employs SMOTE for handling class imbalance, outlier detection for identifying and removing abnormal observations, and hyperparameter tuning to optimize the model's performance. The authors evaluated the proposed framework using various performance metrics and demonstrated its effectiveness in detecting intrusions with other datasets (KDD-Cup 99 and UNSW- NB-15) and conventional classifiers. Furthermore, the proposed framework can protect sensitive data while achieving high intrusion detection performance.

papers.title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
papers.abstract:
The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem has transformed various sectors but has also introduced significant cybersecurity challenges. Traditional centralized security methods often struggle to balance privacy preservation and real-time threat detection in IoT networks. To address these issues, this study proposes a Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework designed specifically for IoT environments. The framework enables decentralized data processing by training models locally on edge devices, ensuring data privacy. Secure aggregation of these locally trained models is achieved using homomorphic encryption, allowing collaborative learning without exposing sensitive information.
  The proposed framework utilizes recurrent neural networks (RNNs) for anomaly detection, optimized for resource-constrained IoT networks. Experimental results demonstrate that the system effectively detects complex cyber threats, including distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, with over 98% accuracy. Additionally, it improves energy efficiency by reducing resource consumption by 20% compared to centralized approaches.
  This research addresses critical gaps in IoT cybersecurity by integrating federated learning with advanced threat detection techniques. The framework offers a scalable and privacy-preserving solution adaptable to various IoT applications. Future work will explore the integration of blockchain for transparent model aggregation and quantum-resistant cryptographic methods to further enhance security in evolving technological landscapes.

papers.title: A review of Federated Learning in Intrusion Detection Systems for IoT
papers.abstract: Intrusion detection systems are evolving into intelligent systems that perform data analysis searching for anomalies in their environment. The development of deep learning technologies opened the door to build more complex and effective threat detection models. However, training those models may be computationally infeasible in most Internet of Things devices. Current approaches rely on powerful centralized servers that receive data from all their parties -- violating basic privacy constraints and substantially affecting response times and operational costs due to the huge communication overheads. To mitigate these issues, Federated Learning emerged as a promising approach where different agents collaboratively train a shared model, neither exposing training data to others nor requiring a compute-intensive centralized infrastructure. This paper focuses on the application of Federated Learning approaches in the field of Intrusion Detection. Both technologies are described in detail and current scientific progress is reviewed and categorized. Finally, the paper highlights the limitations present in recent works and presents some future directions for this technology.

Section IV, 'EMPLOYING FEDERATED LEARNING SYSTEMS FOR INTRUSION DETECTION,' further details existing FL-IDS architectures and approaches, demonstrating that the core idea is already a well-established area of research and implementation.

existing_examples.name: Federated Learning Research (General)
existing_examples.description: Federated learning (FL) is a well-established research area, with numerous papers and projects exploring its application across various domains, including security and privacy-preserving machine learning.

existing_examples.name: Federated Learning for Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS)
existing_examples.description: Specific research papers and prototypes have explored applying federated learning to train IDS models. Examples include works by researchers from Google, universities, and cybersecurity firms focusing on collaborative threat detection without data sharing.

existing_examples.name: Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning (PPML)
existing_examples.description: FL is a subset of PPML. Other techniques like homomorphic encryption and secure multi-party computation are also used for similar goals in collaborative model training.

existing_examples.name: Google's use of Federated Learning
existing_examples.description: Google has been a pioneer in FL, using it for Gboard predictions and other on-device model training, demonstrating its practical application for privacy-preserving learning.

reality_check.assessment: The core idea of using federated learning to train an intrusion detection model across multiple organizations without sharing raw data is a well-known and actively researched area. Numerous academic papers, prototypes, and even some commercial initiatives (though perhaps not yet widely adopted as off-the-shelf products specifically for IDS) have explored this exact concept. The privacy-preserving aspect through collaborative learning is the fundamental premise of federated learning itself.
novelty_aspects: Specific architectural choices for the FL setup in an IDS context (e.g., aggregation strategies, model types)
Novel methods for handling data heterogeneity across different organizations' network traffic
Integration with specific existing IDS frameworks or network monitoring tools
Performance optimization for real-time intrusion detection in a federated setting
Novel incentive mechanisms for organizations to participate in the federated learning process
reality_check.recommendation: While the overarching concept is not novel, there is still significant room for innovation in its specific implementation and optimization. Focus on a unique contribution within this established field, such as developing a highly efficient aggregation algorithm for IDS, addressing specific challenges like data drift in network traffic, or creating a robust, production-ready framework that outperforms existing research prototypes. Clearly articulate what makes your approach superior or uniquely suited to a particular problem or industry.

matched_sections.text_snippet: Federated learning is a technique of decentralized machine learning. that allows multiple parties to collaborate and learn a shared model without sharing their raw data. matched_sections.paper_title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection In Internet Of Vehicles Through Federated Learning
matched_sections.heading: Framework Proposed:
matched_sections.text_snippet: Our study explores the implementation of Federated Learning using two edge devices, namely Internet of Vehicles (IOVs), and a central server. The central server combines the received models and trains a resulting model on its dataset.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Abstract
matched_sections.text_snippet: Traditional centralized security methods often struggle to balance privacy preservation and real-time threat detection in IoT networks. To address these issues, this study proposes a Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework designed specifically for IoT environments.
matched_sections.reason: Both the research idea and the paper identify the problem of balancing privacy and threat detection, and propose federated learning as a solution in a network environment.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Background
matched_sections.text_snippet: Traditional approaches to IoT security often rely on centralized models that aggregate data at a central server for analysis. Moreover, centralized systems inherently compromise user privacy by requiring the transfer of raw data from IoT devices to remote servers. To address these shortcomings, researchers are increasingly exploring decentralized methods that prioritize privacy and scalability.
matched_sections.reason: Both the research idea and the paper highlight the privacy issues with sharing raw data in traditional centralized approaches and suggest decentralized methods like federated learning as a solution.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Federated Learning as a Solution
matched_sections.text_snippet: Federated learning (FL) has emerged as a promising solution for addressing privacy and scalability concerns in distributed systems. By eliminating the need to transmit raw data, FL inherently preserves user privacy and reduces the risk of data leakage.
matched_sections.reason: The paper explicitly states that federated learning is a solution for privacy concerns by eliminating the need to transmit raw data, directly aligning with the user's problem statement.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Abstract
matched_sections.text_snippet: The framework enables decentralized data processing by training models locally on edge devices, ensuring data privacy. Secure aggregation of these locally trained models is achieved using homomorphic encryption, allowing collaborative learning without exposing sensitive information.
matched_sections.reason: The paper describes a federated learning approach that trains models locally and uses secure aggregation for collaborative learning without exposing sensitive information, which is the core of the user's proposed method.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Federated Learning as a Solution
matched_sections.text_snippet: Unlike conventional machine learning models, FL allows decentralized data processing by enabling individual devices to train models locally. The locally trained models are then aggregated to create a global model, ensuring that sensitive data remains on the originating device.
matched_sections.reason: The paper details the federated learning methodology of local model training and aggregation without sharing raw data, which is precisely what the user proposes.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Methods
matched_sections.text_snippet: The framework integrates federated learning (FL), privacy-preserving encryption methods, and anomaly detection techniques using advanced machine learning models. We ensure scalability, computational efficiency, and real-time threat detection, which are critical for IoT networks with limited resources and high vulnerability.
matched_sections.reason: The paper outlines a framework that integrates federated learning and privacy-preserving encryption for threat detection, directly matching the user's proposed method.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Framework Overview
matched_sections.text_snippet: IoT Edge Device Layer : Hosts local model training and threat detection using recurrent neural networks (RNNs) optimized for time-series data generated by IoT devices. Federated Aggregation Layer : Implements a secure aggregation mechanism using homomorphic encryption, enabling decentralized model updates without exposing sensitive data.
matched_sections.reason: The framework overview describes local model training on edge devices and secure aggregation without exposing sensitive data, which is the exact method proposed by the user.

matched_sections.paper_title: Federated Learning-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for IoT Networks with Privacy-Preserving and Real-Time Threat Detection Capabilities
matched_sections.heading: Federated Aggregation with Homomorphic Encryption
matched_sections.text_snippet: Once local models are trained, the updates i W are encrypted using a homomorphic encryption scheme E . This encryption enables arithmetic operations (e.g., addition and multiplication) to be performed directly on encrypted data without decryption. Homomorphic encryption ensures that the aggregated model retains the properties of the individual updates without exposing sensitive information.
matched_sections.reason: The paper explicitly describes using homomorphic encryption for secure aggregation of model updates, allowing collaborative learning without exposing sensitive information, which is a key part of the user's method.
